title: [lichen_util.h:85-100] secure_zero word-size path assumes unsigned long alignment works
description:
Gap: The word-aligned write path assumes all platforms handle word writes correctly.

What: Lines 85-100 perform word-sized writes for performance. The cast on line 93 assumes that after alignment, writing an unsigned long is safe and will be atomic/correct on all target platforms.

Why it matters: On some exotic architectures (not typical ARM Cortex-M), word-aligned writes may still not be atomic, or the volatile qualifier may not behave as expected. While the comment at line 77-83 discusses strict aliasing, it doesn't address potential issues on architectures where sizeof(unsigned long) is not the native word size. Low priority since LICHEN targets Cortex-M where this is safe.
close_reason: Acceptable: As the issue states, LICHEN targets Cortex-M where word-aligned writes are atomic and correct. The code has extensive comments (lines 70-83) explaining the safety model. Exotic architectures are not in scope. Adding ifdefs for hypothetical platforms would be speculative complexity.
